PonteiroData.title: PonteiroData
PonteiroData.description: Represents a single row of extracted data from the 'Ponteiros.html' URL, containing various pointer-related information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PonteiroData entity.

funcao.description: The function or operation associated with the pointer.

sinal.description: The signal or status associated with the pointer.

definition.description: Stores the dynamically extracted and parsed `PonteiroData` records from the external URL. This data is intended to be universally readable, representing the latest available information. Does not require denormalized authorization fields as it's universally accessible for viewing.

params.name: ponteiroDataId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific PonteiroData record, typically corresponding to its 'id' field.

firestore.reasoning:
The core data for PonteiroScope, which consists of dynamically extracted `PonteiroData` records, will be stored in a top-level collection named `/ponteiro_data`. Each document within this collection will represent a single `PonteiroData` entity, using its `id` field as the document ID for efficient lookup and management. This structure adheres to the principle of structural segregation by ensuring all documents in `/ponteiro_data` share the same security posture.

Authorization Independence is achieved because the `PonteiroData` entities are designed to be universally accessible (either public or accessible to all users, including anonymous ones, given the `anonymous` auth provider). There are no hierarchical authorization dependencies; access to a `PonteiroData` document does not depend on attributes of a parent document. Security rules for this collection can be straightforward, granting read access to all users without requiring any `get()` calls to other documents.

This design fully supports Query-as-Permission (QAPs). Since all documents in `/ponteiro_data` are intended to have the same read permissions, a `list` operation on this collection will naturally return all accessible documents. There's no need for server-side filtering, as the security rules themselves define the complete set of visible data. The client-side 'Interactive Data Table' feature will handle presentation logic such as pagination, sorting, and filtering of this uniformly accessible dataset.
